initiative: Automated ClipForge Agent Worker + Render Queue

stories.title: Add DayRenderJob model + additive migration
stories.value: so render jobs have a fitness-day-scoped queue with a claim/draft/approve/render lifecycle, separate from project-only ScheduledItem
ac: DayRenderJob model: id cuid, date (USER_TZ midnight), goalId FK (onDelete: Cascade), status String @default('pending'), claimedAt?, draftRef?, approvedAt?, renderedAt?, outputRef?, errorMessage?, clipforgeProjectId? (nullable column; required at tool layer), payload Json?, createdAt, updatedAt
status values documented in a comment: pending | claimed | drafted | approved | rendering | rendered | failed (all 7; 'rendering' is referenced by A4/A5/B4)
draftRef / outputRef are opaque strings (ClipForge draft id / reel URL-or-id); outputRef format: http(s) -> link else plain text
@@unique([goalId, date]); @@index([goalId, status, date])
Migration additive/Neon-safe; npx prisma migrate dev succeeds; prisma.dayRenderJob generated; tsc clean; Measurement/ScheduledItem untouched

stories.title: Add render-job MCP tools (registerRenderTools)
stories.value: so the GPU-box Claude worker can queue/list/claim/draft/render/complete render jobs over MCP
ac: New registerRenderTools(server) with SIX tools: queue_render_job(date, clipforgeProjectId [required in zod], goalId? -> defaults to current FOCUS goal, error if none) UPSERT (re-queue terminal job -> pending); list_render_jobs(status?, from?, to?); claim_render_job(id) ATOMIC updateMany({where:{id,status:'pending'},data:{status:'claimed',claimedAt}}) returns claimed:boolean from count; start_render_job(id) ATOMIC updateMany({where:{id,status:'approved'},data:{status:'rendering',claimedAt}}) returns started:boolean (the render-run claim, M1); submit_render_draft(id,{draftRef,notes}) -> 'drafted'; complete_render_job(id,{outputRef,status:'rendered'|'failed', errorMessage?})
EXPLICIT AC: registerRenderTools(server) wired into registerAll() (else tools silently absent)
EXPLICIT AC: confirm the new tools appear in tools/list against the live deploy (MCP_SERVER_VERSION auto-bumps via commit SHA — verify connector discovers them)
All safe()-wrapped; dates via parseDateInput (USER_TZ); discoverable descriptions steering the worker
MCP curl: queue -> list -> claim (2nd claim returns claimed:false) -> submit_render_draft -> (approve via UI) -> start_render_job (2nd returns started:false) -> complete_render_job round-trips

stories.title: Add cheap authed peek endpoint GET /api/render-jobs/peek
stories.value: so the GPU-box cron can check for work without spinning up a Claude session (no wasted subscription tokens)
ac: GET /api/render-jobs/peek returns { pendingCount, nextJob:{id,date}|null, approvedCount, nextApprovedJob:{id,date}|null }
Bearer auth via timingSafeEqual pattern from src/app/api/mcp/[token]/route.ts; 401/404 on bad token (no header leak)
Read-only; force-dynamic; curl returns correct shape against seeded jobs

stories.title: Day page: Queue-for-render + Approve-render affordance
stories.value: so I can mark a curated day ready, see its render status, approve the draft, and open the finished reel
ac: Footage card on src/app/days/[dateKey]/page.tsx shows 'Queue for render' (server action -> DayRenderJob; collects/uses clipforgeProjectId) when no active job
Status badge reflects lifecycle (pending/claimed/drafted/approved/rendering/rendered/failed); the 'drafted' badge IS the v1 notification (no push infra)
'Approve render' action on 'drafted' -> status 'approved' + approvedAt; revalidates
'rendered' -> outputRef as external link if http(s) else plain text; 'failed' -> errorMessage shown
ERROR PATHS: queueing when a non-terminal job exists is a no-op with a friendly message; server-action/DB failure surfaces a visible error, not a 500
Server actions revalidatePath the day; renders at 390px

stories.title: Stale-claim reaper for stuck render jobs
stories.value: so a worker that dies mid-job doesn't strand a job in claimed/rendering forever
ac: Jobs in 'claimed' with claimedAt < now-30min reset to 'pending'; jobs in 'rendering' with claimedAt < now-30min reset to 'approved'
Runs inside the GET /api/render-jobs/peek handler (zero new infrastructure) — documented
Idempotent; never reaps terminal (rendered/failed) jobs

stories.title: Document the automated flow in the bridge doc
stories.value: so the manual bridge doc reflects the new queue + lifecycle once Epic A ships
ac: Add an 'Automated flow' section to docs/integrations/goaldmine-clipforge-bridge.md: queue -> claim -> draft -> approve -> render lifecycle + the new render MCP tools
Cross-link docs/roadmap/clipforge-auto-render-plan.md

stories.title: [spec] GPU-box worker scaffold + subscription auth (API-key fallback)
stories.value: so the box can run headless Claude on the Max subscription, with a one-env-var switch to the paid API
ac: Spec: worker repo/dir layout (outside goaldmine); env (GOALDMINE_MCP url+token, ClipForge mcp config, claude auth)
Headless Claude Code (claude -p --output-format stream-json --mcp-config <Goaldmine remote + ClipForge local>) on subscription auth

RISK (R3): subscription OAuth token expires; standing automated use may exceed Max interactive intent -> '$0 beyond Max' is a goal, not a guarantee; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY swap = one env var; expiry detection + alert documented

stories.title: [spec] Cron poller -> peek -> launch correct run
stories.value: so work is picked up automatically without the worker ever long-polling
ac: Spec: cron hits /peek; pendingCount>0 -> launch DRAFT run for nextJob.id; approvedCount>0 -> launch RENDER run for nextApprovedJob.id; else exit
Each launch start-do-exit (R2 split-run); never waits on human approval
Worker never computes dates (R8) — all dates come from MCP responses verbatim

stories.title: [spec] Draft-run automated bridge prompt
stories.value: so a queued day auto-builds a draft reel + caption for my approval
ac: Spec prompt: claim_render_job -> get_day_footage -> list_spines (capture exact slot labels, verify ClipForge tool names against live schema) -> list_assets(clipforgeProjectId) resolve filename->assetId (capturedAt tiebreaker) -> apply_spine -> frame_strip -> caption -> submit_render_draft -> EXIT (no render)
Failure policy (R9): EPS_GUARD -> retry next spine once then fail; POOL_CYCLED -> proceed + annotate draft notes; unresolved filenames -> complete_render_job status='failed' with the list
Multiple highlight markers tie-broken capturedAt asc

stories.title: [spec] Render-run: approved -> render -> write back
stories.value: so an approved draft is rendered unattended and the reel reference lands back in Goaldmine
ac: Spec: start_render_job(id) (approved->rendering, M1) -> ClipForge render(projectId, preset) -> complete_render_job(id,{outputRef, status:'rendered'})
Render error -> complete_render_job status='failed' + errorMessage; exits either way
outputRef = reel URL/id in the documented format

stories.title: [spec] Run-as-a-service + logging + retry
stories.value: so the worker survives reboots and failures are observable
ac: Spec: launchd/systemd unit; per-run logs; bounded retry on transient ClipForge/MCP errors; coordinates with the server-side reaper (A5)
Concurrency guard: only one run per job id at a time

stories.title: [spec] Spike: subscription-authed coaching chat proxy
stories.value: so we know if an in-app coaching window can run on the Max subscription before building UI
ac: Spec/spike: a local Agent SDK or Claude Code process (Goaldmine MCP tools, subscription auth) that takes a chat turn and streams a reply
Confirms streaming transport (SSE/stream-json) + auth viability; reuses B1 auth
Documents it is a SEPARATE service from the batch render worker (R11)

stories.precondition: MUST be split into multiple stories before handing to /feature-dev — do not build as one unit
stories.title: [spec] In-app streaming coaching window UI (EPIC — split before build)
stories.value: so I can chat with the coach inside Goaldmine instead of copy-pasting prompts
ac: Spec: streaming chat UI augmenting static /coach; backend proxies each turn to the C1 process; renders streamed tokens
Graceful offline state when the worker isn't running

stories.title: [spec] Coaching-window risk + usage-policy doc
stories.value: so the personal-use / cap / TOS constraints are explicit before any build
ac: Doc: personal single-user only (distribution needs the paid API); bound by Max usage/daily caps; offline when PC off; subscription-auth/TOS risk (R3)
Decision gate: proceed on subscription vs paid API
